selectedAssertions.label: Actuation exposes protocol profiles for visual, haptic, and stim lanes
selectedAssertions.detail: device transport should negotiate against explicit protocol profiles instead of a generic opaque bridge

selectedAssertions.label: Actuation adapters surface a live bridge link while a device transport is attached
selectedAssertions.target: haptic-rig bridge ready under session scope
selectedAssertions.detail: adapter policy lanes should expose whether a live device transport is currently attached

selectedAssertions.label: Actuation dispatch can complete over the live device bridge with protocol-aware acknowledgement
selectedAssertions.target: one bridge dispatch / protocol-aware bridge transport / acknowledged
selectedAssertions.detail: the first concrete hardware-transport slice should prove protocol-aware acked bridge delivery instead of only file fallback

selectedAssertions.label: Actuation dispatch can complete over UDP/OSC with a protocol-aware encoded command frame
selectedAssertions.target: udp-osc transport / visual protocol frame / encoded command payload
selectedAssertions.detail: the first concrete device transport slice should prove protocol-aware delivery over a non-bridge transport, not only websocket-mediated acks

selectedAssertions.label: Supervised serial transport registration exposes vendor/device identity and heartbeat policy
selectedAssertions.target: registered serial-json transport with vendor/model and heartbeat policy
selectedAssertions.detail: vendor/device lanes should be durable registry entries with explicit supervision policy, not anonymous direct writes

selectedAssertions.label: Serial transport heartbeat records capability health and raises the device to healthy
selectedAssertions.target: healthy heartbeat with full capability coverage
selectedAssertions.detail: direct vendor transports should advertise device liveness and capability coverage before they are eligible for low-latency delivery

selectedAssertions.label: Actuation dispatch can complete over the supervised serial vendor transport
selectedAssertions.target: serial-json delivery with persisted vendor payload
selectedAssertions.detail: the first vendor-specific direct lane should prove concrete delivery without requiring the websocket bridge

selectedAssertions.label: Stale serial heartbeat isolates only the affected device and forces fallback
selectedAssertions.target: isolated transport with file fallback on stale heartbeat
selectedAssertions.detail: per-device fault isolation should contain a stale device without suppressing the rest of the adapter lane

selectedAssertions.label: Operator reset plus renewed heartbeat restores the isolated serial transport
selectedAssertions.target: reset clears isolation, heartbeat restores health, direct serial delivery resumes
selectedAssertions.detail: device supervision should allow controlled recovery after an isolated fault instead of requiring transport re-registration

selectedAssertions.label: HTTP/2 direct transport registration exposes a supervised RPC-class device endpoint
selectedAssertions.target: registered http2-json transport with vendor/model and heartbeat policy
selectedAssertions.detail: the next direct device lane should be a typed RPC-class endpoint, not just another file or datagram bridge

selectedAssertions.label: HTTP/2 direct transport heartbeat establishes healthy low-latency readiness
selectedAssertions.target: healthy heartbeat with low latency and full capability coverage
selectedAssertions.detail: RPC-class direct lanes should prove liveness and capability coverage before they are eligible to outrank other transports

selectedAssertions.label: Transport selection prefers the healthiest lowest-latency direct lane for haptic delivery
selectedAssertions.target: http2-json delivery selected over other healthy haptic transports
selectedAssertions.detail: direct transport routing should use health and latency as a real preference signal instead of registry insertion order
